newsSince: Two offsetting developments since June 9: (1) prosecutors raided Samsung Electronics HQ on June 11 in an insider-trading probe tied to Rainbow becoming a Samsung subsidiary, and questioned founder Oh Jun-ho — a governance/overhang negative; (2) on June 15 Rainbow placed its RB-Y1 mobile dual-arm robot in a Coupang fulfillment center for a pilot, its first reported move from lab to a live commercial floor — a genuine commercialization data point.

sources.label: TechTimes: Rainbow RB-Y1 in Coupang warehouse
Seoul Economic: Prosecutors raid Samsung over Rainbow insider trading
SeoulEconomicDaily: prosecutors raid Samsung over Rainbow insider probe
TechTimes: RB-Y1 dual-arm robot in Coupang warehouse

verdicts.rankRationale: Mixed since June 9: Coupang RB-Y1 pilot is a genuine commercialization positive, offset by a Samsung-stake insider-trading raid that pressures sentiment (overhang is on Samsung, not Rainbow's tech). Net neutral to thesis; still a crowded, already-repriced direct humanoid name. Hold.
verdicts.bull: Samsung-backed direct humanoid builder with live warehouse deployment (Coupang); strategic factory-automation roadmap to 2030.
verdicts.neutral: Real commercialization progress but offset by governance noise; valuation already rich.
verdicts.bear: Insider-trading probe clouds the Samsung relationship; crowded, expensive direct-humanoid beta.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Scaling RB-Y1 deployments and Samsung factory robotics rollout.
